findings.summary: Existing tool architecture uses Effect.gen, Tool.define, global 5-permit semaphore, Effect.orDie for all errors, minimal span propagation, unbounded cache growth, and no typed error hierarchy

9 anti-patterns identified: orDie-everything, side-effects-in-init, bare-string-outputs, one-global-semaphore, unbounded-cache, adhoc-validation, mixing-execution-and-result-metadata, deeply-nested-generators, module-scope-mutable-state

New tool template with 9-step checklist and canonical skeleton covering schema, formatValidationError, cacheable, maxConcurrency, structured logging, timeouts, Error result handling, and registry integration

3-phase growth plan: Phase 1 (50 tools) focuses on error hierarchy and semaphore split; Phase 2 (100 tools) adds per-tool concurrency, circuit breakers, tag-based cache; Phase 3 (200+ tools) adds dynamic discovery, weighted pools, tool health monitoring
